title: Sarga 107
summary: Rama's divine birth, childhood, protection of Sage Vishwamitra's rituals from demons, breaking of Shiva's bow, and marriage to Sita. Addresses youth, childhood, education, overcoming early obstacles, and marriage.

sections.summary: This passage begins with “Having sent Lakshmana away, Rama, filled with sorrow and grief, spoke these words to his chief priest, ministers, and citizens.” and develops toward “Then all the subjects spoke to Rama, saying, "We will follow you, wherever you, Rama, will go."”

This passage begins with “O Rama, if you truly have unsurpassed affection and love for your citizens, then let us all, with our children and wives, accompany you on your path.” and develops toward “After consecrating his heroic sons and establishing them in their cities, he then dispatched messengers to the great-souled Shatrughna. इत्यार्षे…”

verses.sanskrit: विसृज्य लक्ष्मणं रामो दुःखशोकसमन्वितः । पुरोधसं मन्त्रिणश्च नैगमांश्चेदमब्रवीत् ।। 7.107.1 ।।
verses.translation: Having sent Lakshmana away, Rama, filled with sorrow and grief, spoke these words to his chief priest, ministers, and citizens.

verses.sanskrit: अद्य राज्ये ऽभिषेक्ष्यामि भरतं धर्मवत्सलम् । अयोध्यायाः पतिं वीरं ततो यास्याम्यहं वनम् ।। 7.107.2 ।।
verses.translation: Rama declared, "Today I shall install the righteous and brave Bharata as the ruler of Ayodhya, and then I myself shall go to the forest."

verses.sanskrit: प्रवेशयत सम्भारान्माभूत्कालस्य पर्ययः । अद्यैवाहं गमिष्यामि लक्ष्मणेन गतां गतिम् ।। 7.107.3 ।।
verses.translation: Bring in the preparations, let there be no delay, for today itself I will follow the path that Lakshmana has taken.

verses.sanskrit: तच्छ्रुत्वा राघवेणोक्तं सर्वाः प्रकृतयो भृशम् । मूर्धभिः प्रणता भूमौ गतसत्त्वा इवाभवन् ।। 7.107.4 ।।
verses.translation: Having heard those words spoken by Rama, all the subjects greatly bowed their heads to the ground, as if they had lost their very life.

verses.sanskrit: भरतश्च विसञ्ज्ञो ऽभूच्छ्रुत्वा रामस्य भाषितम् । राज्यं विगर्हयामास राघवं चेदमब्रवीत् ।। 7.107.5 ।।
verses.translation: Bharata also became unconscious upon hearing Rama's words, and condemning kingship, he spoke these words to Rama.

verses.sanskrit: सत्येनाहं शपे राजन्स्वर्गलोके न चैव हि । न कामये यथा राज्यं त्वां विना रघुनन्दन ।। 7.107.6 ।।
verses.translation: O King, I swear by truth, and indeed by the heavenly world, that I do not desire kingship without you, O delight of the Raghus.

verses.sanskrit: इमौ कुशलवौ राजन्नभिषिञ्च नराधिप । कोसलेषु कुशं वीरमुत्तरेषु तथा लवम् ।। 7.107.7 ।।
verses.translation: O King, O lord of men, enthrone these two, Kusha and Lava; Kusha, the brave, in Kosala, and similarly Lava in the northern regions.

verses.sanskrit: शत्रुघ्नस्य तु गच्छन्तु दूतास्त्वरितविक्रमाः । इदं गमनमस्माकं स्वर्गायाख्यातु मा चिरम् ।। 7.107.8 ।।
verses.translation: Let swift messengers go to Shatrughna and without delay inform him of our departure to heaven.

verses.sanskrit: तच्छ्रुत्वा भरतेनोक्तं दृष्ट्वा चापि ह्यधोमुखान् । पौरान्दुःखेन सन्तप्तान्वसिष्ठो वाक्यमब्रवीत् ।। 7.107.9 ।।
verses.translation: Having heard what Bharata said and seeing the citizens distressed and downcast with sorrow, Vasishtha spoke these words.

verses.sanskrit: वत्स राम इमाः पश्य धरणीं प्रकृतीर्गताः । ज्ञात्वैषामीप्सितं कार्यं मा चैषां विप्रियं कृथाः ।। 7.107.10 ।।
verses.translation: Vasishtha said, "Dear Rama, behold these subjects fallen to the ground; understanding their desire, do not cause them any displeasure."

verses.sanskrit: वसिष्ठस्य तु वाक्येन उत्थाप्य प्रकृतीजनम् । किं करोमीति काकुत्स्थः सर्वा वचनमब्रवीत् ।। 7.107.11 ।।
verses.translation: And by the words of Vasishtha, Rama, having raised up the people, spoke to all, asking, "What shall I do?"

verses.sanskrit: ततः सर्वाः प्रकृतयो रामं वचनमब्रुवन् । गच्छन्तमनुगच्छामो यत्र राम गमिष्यसि ।। 7.107.12 ।।
verses.translation: Then all the subjects spoke to Rama, saying, "We will follow you, wherever you, Rama, will go."

verses.sanskrit: पौरेषु यदि ते प्रीतिर्यदि स्नेहो ह्यनुत्तमः । सपुत्रदाराः काकुत्स्थ समागच्छाम सत्पथम् ।। 7.107.13 ।।
verses.translation: O Rama, if you truly have unsurpassed affection and love for your citizens, then let us all, with our children and wives, accompany you on your path.

verses.sanskrit: तपोवनं वा दुर्गं वा नदीमम्भोनिधिं तथा । वयं ते यदि न त्याज्याः सर्वान्नो नय ईश्वर ।। 7.107.14 ।।
verses.translation: O Lord, if we are not to be abandoned by you, then lead all of us, whether to a hermitage, a fortress, a river, or the ocean.

verses.sanskrit: एषा नः परमा प्रीतिरेष नः परमो वरः । हृद्गता नः सदा प्रीतिस्तवानुगमने नृप ।। 7.107.15 ।।
verses.translation: O King, this is our supreme affection and our greatest wish, for our hearts are always filled with love for following you.

verses.sanskrit: पौराणां दृढभक्तिं च बाढमित्येव सो ऽब्रवीत् । स्वकृतान्तं चान्ववेक्ष्य तस्मिन्नहनि राघवः ।। 7.107.16 ।।
verses.translation: Having observed the firm devotion of the citizens and considering his own destined end, Rama said, "Certainly, yes," on that very day.

verses.sanskrit: कोसलेषु कुशं वीरमुत्तरेषु तथा लवम् । अभिषिच्य महात्मानावुभौ रामः कुशीलवौ ।। 7.107.17 ।।
verses.translation: Rama, having consecrated both the brave and great-souled Kusha in Kosala and similarly Lava in the northern regions, completed his arrangements.

verses.sanskrit: अभिषिक्तौ सुतावङ्के प्रतिष्ठाप्य पुरे ततः । परिष्वज्य महाबाहुर्मूर्ध्न्युपाघ्राय चासकृत् ।। 7.107.18 ।।
verses.translation: Then, the mighty-armed Rama, having consecrated his two sons and established them in the city, repeatedly embraced them and kissed them on their heads.

verses.sanskrit: रथानां तु सहस्राणि नागानामयुतानि च । दशायुतानि चाश्वानामेकैकस्य धनं ददौ ।। 7.107.19 ।।
verses.translation: He gave to each of his sons thousands of chariots, ten thousands of elephants, and one hundred thousand horses as wealth.

verses.sanskrit: बहुरत्नौ बहुधनौ हृष्टपुष्टजनावृतौ । स्वे पुरे प्रेषयामास भ्रातरौ तु कुशीलवौ ।। 7.107.20 ।।
verses.translation: He then sent the two brothers, Kusha and Lava, to their respective cities, each endowed with many jewels, much wealth, and surrounded by happy and prosperous people.

verses.sanskrit: अभिषिच्य सुतौ वीरौ प्रतिष्ठाप्य पुरे तदा । दूतान्सम्प्रेषयामास शत्रुघ्नाय महात्मने ।। 7.107.21 ।।
verses.translation: After consecrating his heroic sons and establishing them in their cities, he then dispatched messengers to the great-souled Shatrughna. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीये आदिकाव्ये श्रीमदुत्तरकाण्डे सप्तोत्तरशततमः सर्गः ।। 107 ।। Thus ends the hundred and seventh sarga of Uttara Kanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.
